_meta.comment: TRIKAL VAANI - CEO PROTECTION HEADER - festivals.json v1.0 - Rohiit Gupta - Chief Vedic Architect - 50 festivals for /events/[slug] route - Phase B2

festivals.name: Makar Sankranti
festivals.description: The day Sun enters Capricorn (Makar Rashi), marking Uttarayan — the auspicious northward journey of the Sun.
festivals.significance: Most powerful day for Surya worship, donation, and spiritual practice. Marks the end of inauspicious Dhanurmas.
festivals.muhurat: Punya Kaal: Sunrise to 12:30 PM IST
dos: Take a holy bath in sacred rivers (Ganga, Yamuna)
Offer Arghya to Surya at sunrise
donts: Do not engage in conflicts or harsh speech

festivals.name: Republic Day Panchang
festivals.description: India's Republic Day. Auspicious day for national prosperity prayers and patriotic spiritual practice.
festivals.significance: Strong Sun energy day. Ideal for new beginnings related to civic duty and leadership.
dos: Hoist the national flag with reverence
Pray for national peace and prosperity
donts: Avoid negative speech about the nation

festivals.name: Vasant Panchami
festivals.description: Day dedicated to Maa Saraswati, goddess of knowledge, music, art, and wisdom. Marks the arrival of spring.
festivals.significance: Best day to begin education, music, art, or any knowledge pursuit. Children's first writing (Akshar Abhyasa) is performed.
dos: Worship Maa Saraswati with yellow flowers
Begin new learning, music, or art
Place books and instruments before the goddess
donts: Do not insult books or knowledge

festivals.name: Maha Shivratri
festivals.description: The Great Night of Shiva. Most powerful night for Shiva worship, meditation, and spiritual awakening.
festivals.significance: The night when Shiva performs the Tandava. Fasting and Jaagran on this night brings moksha-level spiritual benefits.
festivals.muhurat: Nishita Kaal: 11:30 PM Feb 15 to 12:30 AM Feb 16 IST. Four prahar puja through the night.
dos: Perform Rudrabhishek with milk, water, bel patra
Stay awake and meditate during the night
donts: Do not sleep during the night

festivals.name: Holika Dahan
festivals.description: The bonfire night before Holi, symbolizing the burning of evil and triumph of devotion (Prahlad's victory).
festivals.significance: Sacred fire ritual that destroys negative karma and protects from evil eye and obstacles.
festivals.muhurat: Pradosh Kaal: 6:30 PM to 8:45 PM IST
dos: Offer coconut, grains, and sweets to the fire
donts: Avoid lighting fire at inauspicious time (Bhadra Kaal)
Do not perform Holika Dahan during Bhadra

festivals.name: Holi
festivals.description: The festival of colors celebrating divine love and the arrival of spring.
festivals.significance: Day to dissolve enmity, embrace joy, and celebrate Krishna's playful spirit. Resets relationships and energy.
festivals.muhurat: Sunrise to 12:30 PM IST for color play
dos: Visit temples and seek blessings of elders
Forgive and reconcile with estranged family

festivals.name: Chaitra Navratri
festivals.planetary_ruler: Maa Durga and her nine forms
festivals.description: Nine sacred nights dedicated to nine forms of Maa Durga. Beginning of the Hindu New Year.
festivals.significance: Most powerful period for spiritual sadhana, fasting, and invoking Shakti energy. Each day worships a different form.
festivals.muhurat: Ghatasthapana: 6:30 AM to 9:00 AM IST on March 19
dos: Perform Kanya Pujan on Ashtami or Navami
donts: Do not cut hair or nails during these 9 days

festivals.name: Ram Navami
festivals.description: Birthday of Lord Rama, the seventh avatar of Vishnu. Celebrated on the ninth day of Chaitra Navratri.
festivals.significance: Marks the descent of dharma into the world. Powerful day for invoking righteousness and family harmony.
dos: Recite Sundara Kanda or Ram Raksha Stotra
Perform Ram Janma Aarti at noon
donts: Do not engage in family disputes

festivals.name: Hanuman Jayanti
festivals.description: Birthday of Lord Hanuman, the supreme devotee of Rama and remover of obstacles.
festivals.significance: Most powerful day to remove enemies, fears, and obstacles. Strengthens Mars in birth chart.
festivals.muhurat: Sunrise to sunset, especially Tuesday-aligned hours
dos: Offer red sindoor, jasmine oil, boondi

festivals.name: Akshaya Tritiya
festivals.description: The 'never-diminishing' day. One of the most auspicious days, where any good deed brings eternal results.
festivals.significance: Best day for buying gold, starting new ventures, marriage, and major investments. Treasure trove of cosmic blessings.
festivals.muhurat: Sunrise to 12:30 PM IST is most auspicious
donts: Do not lend money on this day

festivals.name: Buddha Purnima
festivals.description: Birth, enlightenment, and Mahaparinirvana of Lord Buddha — all on the same Vaisakha Purnima.
festivals.significance: Triple-blessed full moon. Powerful day for meditation, compassion, and Vipassana practice.
dos: Donate to monks and the needy
donts: Do not consume meat or alcohol

festivals.name: Ganga Saptami
festivals.description: The day Maa Ganga descended to earth from the matted locks of Lord Shiva.
festivals.significance: Bath in Ganga or sprinkling Ganga jal on this day washes away lifetimes of sins.
dos: Take holy bath in Ganga or sprinkle Ganga jal
Donate water pots to the thirsty
donts: Do not pollute any water body

festivals.name: Vat Savitri Vrat
festivals.description: Married women fast and worship the banyan tree (Vat Vriksha) for the long life of their husbands.
festivals.significance: Commemorates Savitri's victory over Yama to bring her husband Satyavan back to life.
dos: Tie sacred thread around banyan tree (108 times)
donts: Do not break the fast prematurely

festivals.name: Jagannath Rath Yatra
festivals.description: Lord Jagannath, with Balabhadra and Subhadra, travels on chariots from Puri temple to Gundicha temple.
festivals.significance: The only day Lord Jagannath leaves his sanctum. Pulling the chariot is said to grant moksha.
festivals.muhurat: Throughout the day, especially during chariot pulling hours
dos: Pull or touch the rath rope if possible
Sing Jagannath bhajans and Hare Krishna mantra
donts: Do not eat food before darshan

festivals.name: Guru Purnima
festivals.description: Sacred day to honor one's spiritual guru and Maharshi Vyasa, who compiled the Vedas.
festivals.significance: Most powerful day to receive spiritual transmission from Guru. Strengthens Jupiter in birth chart.
dos: Touch the feet of your Guru
donts: Do not break promises made to Guru

festivals.name: Hariyali Teej
festivals.description: Festival celebrating the union of Shiva and Parvati. Married women pray for marital bliss.
festivals.significance: Welcomes the monsoon and celebrates Parvati's penance to win Shiva. Strengthens Venus and Moon.

festivals.name: Raksha Bandhan
festivals.description: Sacred bond between brothers and sisters. Sister ties Rakhi for brother's protection.
festivals.significance: Strengthens family Karma, bond, and mutual protection. Mercury energy peak day.
festivals.muhurat: Avoid Bhadra Kaal. Best: 1:30 PM to 9:00 PM IST
dos: Sister ties Rakhi on brother's right wrist
Brother gives gift and protection vow
Apply tilak with kumkum and rice
donts: Do not tie Rakhi during Bhadra Kaal
Avoid family disputes on this day

festivals.name: Krishna Janmashtami
festivals.description: Birthday of Lord Krishna, the eighth avatar of Vishnu, born at midnight on Ashtami.
festivals.significance: Most beloved festival celebrating divine love, dharma, and the supreme personality of Godhead.
festivals.muhurat: Nishita Kaal: 11:45 PM Sept 4 to 12:30 AM Sept 5 IST
dos: Observe fast and break at midnight
Perform Krishna Janma Aarti at midnight
donts: Avoid grains during fast (eat phalahar only)

festivals.name: Ganesh Chaturthi
festivals.description: Birthday of Lord Ganesha, remover of obstacles and lord of beginnings.
festivals.significance: Most powerful day to invoke success, remove obstacles, and begin new ventures.
dos: Offer modak, durva grass, red flowers
donts: Do not look at the moon on this day (avoids false accusations)
Avoid Tulsi leaves in Ganesha worship
Do not break the murti carelessly

festivals.name: Pitru Paksha (Shraddha)
festivals.description: 16-day period dedicated to honoring deceased ancestors through Tarpan and Shraddha rituals.
festivals.significance: Most powerful period to clear ancestral karma (Pitru Dosha). Departed souls visit during this fortnight.
festivals.muhurat: Madhyahna: 11:30 AM to 1:30 PM IST daily
dos: Perform Tarpan with til, water, kusha grass
Feed Brahmins, cows, crows, and dogs
donts: Avoid new purchases or auspicious starts
Do not cut hair or nails
Do not refuse food to anyone who asks

festivals.name: Sharad Navratri
festivals.description: Nine-night autumn festival worshipping nine forms of Maa Durga. Most celebrated Navratri.
festivals.significance: Powerful Shakti period. Each day's color and form brings specific blessings.
festivals.muhurat: Ghatasthapana: 6:15 AM to 9:30 AM IST on Oct 12
donts: Do not cut hair or shave

festivals.name: Durga Ashtami
festivals.description: Eighth day of Sharad Navratri, dedicated to Maa Mahagauri.
festivals.significance: Most powerful day to invoke Maa Durga's protection. Sandhi Puja occurs at the junction of Ashtami-Navami.
festivals.muhurat: Sandhi Puja: Last 24 minutes of Ashtami + first 24 minutes of Navami
donts: Do not break fast before Sandhi Puja

festivals.name: Vijayadashami (Dussehra)
festivals.description: Day Lord Rama defeated Ravana. Victory of dharma over adharma. Celebrated as Dussehra.
festivals.significance: Most auspicious day for new beginnings, weapon worship (Shastra Puja), and burning of ego.
festivals.muhurat: Vijay Muhurat: 2:00 PM to 2:45 PM IST
dos: Burn Ravana effigy or visit a Ramlila
Begin new ventures, especially during Vijay Muhurat
donts: Do not engage in adharmic acts

festivals.name: Karwa Chauth
festivals.description: Married women observe Nirjala fast (without water) for the long life of their husbands.
festivals.significance: Strongest fast for marital bliss. Moon sighting through sieve is essential ritual.
dos: Sight moon through sieve, then look at husband
Husband gives first sip of water
donts: Do not break fast before moon sighting
Do not wear black or white

festivals.name: Dhanteras
festivals.description: First day of Diwali festival. Day of wealth, health, and Lord Dhanvantari (god of Ayurveda).
festivals.significance: Most auspicious day for buying gold, silver, utensils, and starting new financial ventures.
festivals.muhurat: Pradosh Kaal: 6:00 PM to 8:30 PM IST
dos: Buy gold, silver, or new utensils
donts: Do not lend money on this day
Do not refuse anyone who comes asking

festivals.name: Diwali (Deepawali)
festivals.planetary_ruler: Maa Lakshmi, Lord Ganesha, Lord Kuber
festivals.description: The festival of lights. Lakshmi Puja night when Maa Lakshmi visits clean, lit homes.
festivals.significance: Most powerful night for wealth invocation. Marks Lord Rama's return to Ayodhya after 14 years of exile.
festivals.muhurat: Pradosh Kaal Lakshmi Puja: 5:45 PM to 8:15 PM IST. Mahanishita: 11:30 PM to 12:30 AM IST
dos: Open windows and doors at midnight to welcome Lakshmi
donts: Do not gamble (despite tradition — it brings Rahu losses)
Do not lend or borrow money

festivals.name: Govardhan Puja
festivals.description: Day after Diwali. Krishna lifted Govardhan hill to protect Vrindavan from Indra's wrath.
festivals.significance: Day to honor nature, cows, and the protective principle of Krishna.
dos: Make Govardhan from cow dung or food
Offer Annakut (mountain of food) to Krishna
donts: Do not harm any cow or bull

festivals.name: Bhai Dooj
festivals.description: Sister applies tilak on brother's forehead and prays for his long life. Yamuna welcomed Yamraj on this day.
festivals.significance: Strengthens sibling bond. Brothers visiting sisters on this day are protected from untimely death.
dos: Sister offers food, brother gives gift
Pray to Yamraj for long life
donts: Brother should not eat at home this day, only at sister's

festivals.name: Tulsi Vivah
festivals.description: Ceremonial marriage of Tulsi (basil) plant with Lord Shaligram (Vishnu form). Marks beginning of marriage season.
festivals.significance: Auspicious start to wedding season. Brings marital harmony and Vishnu's blessings.
festivals.muhurat: Pradosh Kaal: 5:30 PM to 8:00 PM IST
donts: Do not pluck Tulsi leaves on this day

festivals.name: Kartik Purnima
festivals.description: Full moon of Kartik month. Most spiritually charged Purnima of the year.
festivals.significance: Bath in Ganga, lighting deepdaan, and Satyanarayan Vrat bring multifold blessings. Tripurari Purnima for Shiva devotees.
dos: Take holy bath in Ganga or sacred river
donts: Do not engage in harsh speech or thoughts

festivals.name: Vivah Panchami
festivals.description: Marriage anniversary of Lord Rama and Mata Sita. Celebrated with grand ceremonies in Ayodhya and Janakpur.
festivals.significance: Powerful day for invoking marital harmony and finding suitable life partner.
dos: Donate to newlyweds or unmarried girls
donts: Avoid scheduling weddings on this day (controversial in tradition)

festivals.name: Geeta Jayanti
festivals.description: Day Lord Krishna spoke the Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra.
festivals.significance: Most powerful day to read or begin study of Bhagavad Gita. Brings divine wisdom and clarity.
dos: Read at least one chapter of Bhagavad Gita
Reflect on dharma in own life
donts: Do not engage in adharmic actions

festivals.name: Amavasya (New Moon)
festivals.description: The dark new moon night. Most sacred day for ancestor worship and removing negative karma.
festivals.significance: Powerful for Tarpan, Pitru rituals, and clearing ancestral debts. Also linked to Tantric sadhana.
dos: Take holy bath in sacred river

festivals.name: Purnima (Full Moon)
festivals.description: Full moon night. Peak lunar energy for spiritual practice, fasting, and Satyanarayan Vrat.
festivals.significance: Most fertile day for manifestation, gratitude, and emotional healing.
donts: Do not sleep during peak moon hours

festivals.name: Ekadashi (11th Tithi)
festivals.description: Eleventh day of lunar fortnight. Most sacred day of the month for Vishnu devotees.
festivals.significance: Fasting on Ekadashi grants liberation and clears karma of 1000 lifetimes.

festivals.name: Pradosh Vrat
festivals.description: Trayodashi tithi. Twilight hour fast for Lord Shiva.
festivals.significance: Pradosh Kaal worship of Shiva grants 100x merit. Different days have specific benefits (Som Pradosh for marital harmony, Bhaum for debt removal).
festivals.muhurat: Pradosh Kaal: 1.5 hours before sunset to 1 hour after
donts: Do not break fast before Pradosh Kaal

festivals.name: Shravan Somvar
festivals.description: Mondays of Shravan month. Most powerful Mondays for Shiva worship.
festivals.significance: Each Shravan Monday strengthens Moon and Shiva blessings. Fast brings marriage, progeny, prosperity.
dos: Offer water, milk, bel patra to Shivling
donts: Do not consume onion or garlic

festivals.name: Naga Panchami
festivals.description: Day to worship serpent deities. Removes Kal Sarpa Dosha and snake-related curses.
festivals.significance: Most powerful day for Sarpa Dosha remedies. Snakes are seen as Shiva's ornaments.
donts: Do not harm any snake or insect
Avoid digging earth on this day
Do not cook on iron tava

festivals.name: Chhath Puja
festivals.description: Four-day festival worshipping the Sun. Most rigorous fast in Hindu tradition (36 hours waterless).
festivals.significance: Most powerful Surya worship. Grants progeny, health, prosperity. Sacred to Bihar/UP/Jharkhand.
festivals.muhurat: Sunset (Sandhya Arghya) and Sunrise (Usha Arghya) on Saptami
dos: Offer Arghya to Sun at sunset and sunrise
donts: Do not break Nirjala fast prematurely
Avoid leather goods near puja area

festivals.name: Masik Shivratri
festivals.description: Monthly Chaturdashi tithi of Krishna Paksha. Mini Shivratri every month.
festivals.significance: Each monthly Shivratri brings cumulative Shiva blessings, especially when observed for 14 months.
festivals.muhurat: Nishita Kaal: 11:30 PM to 12:30 AM IST
donts: Do not sleep during night puja

festivals.name: Jaya Ekadashi
festivals.description: Special Ekadashi that grants victory (jaya) over enemies and obstacles.
festivals.significance: Fasting clears curse-karma and grants victory in battles, courts, and competitions.

festivals.name: Putrada Ekadashi
festivals.description: Special Ekadashi for childless couples seeking the boon of progeny.
festivals.significance: Most powerful Ekadashi for fertility and child blessings. Two occur per year.
donts: Do not engage in marital discord

festivals.name: Nirjala Ekadashi
festivals.description: The most rigorous Ekadashi — observed without water for 24 hours.
festivals.significance: Single observance grants merit of all 24 yearly Ekadashis. Bhima of Mahabharata kept only this one.
donts: Do not break vow due to convenience

festivals.name: Guru Pradosh
festivals.description: Pradosh Vrat falling on Thursday. Combines Shiva and Jupiter energies.
festivals.significance: Powerful for Pitru Dosha removal and ancestor blessings. Jupiter strengthens significantly.
festivals.muhurat: Pradosh Kaal: 6:00 PM to 8:30 PM IST
donts: Do not break fast before Pradosh Kaal

festivals.name: Shani Jayanti
festivals.description: Birthday of Lord Shani, the planet of karma and justice.
festivals.significance: Most powerful day to remedy Shani Sade Sati, Shani Dhaiya, and bad karma effects.
festivals.muhurat: Sunrise to sunset, especially Saturday hour
dos: Visit Shani temple, offer mustard oil, til
Donate to laborers, beggars, the poor
Recite Shani Chalisa or Dasharatha Krit Shani Stotra

festivals.name: Hartalika Teej
festivals.description: Most rigorous fast for unmarried girls and married women. Parvati's fast to win Shiva.
festivals.significance: Grants ideal husband to unmarried girls. Married women get long-lived spouse and bliss.
dos: Worship Shiva-Parvati murti made of clay
Stay awake all night singing bhajans
donts: Strictly no water or food for 24 hours
Do not sleep during the night

festivals.name: Anant Chaturdashi
festivals.description: Final day of Ganesh Chaturthi (Visarjan day) and dedicated to Anant Vishnu.
festivals.significance: Tying Anant sutra with 14 knots brings 14 years of protection from troubles.
dos: Worship Lord Vishnu in Anant form
donts: Do not remove Anant sutra carelessly

festivals.name: Dev Uthani Ekadashi
festivals.description: The day Lord Vishnu wakes from his 4-month yogic slumber (Chaturmas ends).
festivals.significance: Marks the start of marriage season and auspicious activities. Most awaited Ekadashi for weddings.
dos: Worship Lord Vishnu, wake him with conch and bells
donts: Do not engage in adharmic acts

festivals.name: Mauni Amavasya
festivals.description: Magh Amavasya observed in silence (Maun). Most powerful Amavasya of the year.
festivals.significance: Bath in Triveni Sangam at Prayagraj on this day grants moksha. Most sacred during Kumbh Mela years.
dos: Observe silence (Maun Vrat) the full day
Take holy bath in Ganga or sacred river
donts: Strictly no speech if observing Maun
Do not engage in worldly affairs

festivals.name: Varuthini Ekadashi
festivals.description: Vaishakh Krishna Ekadashi. Grants protection (varuthini means armor) and prosperity.
festivals.significance: Equivalent to 1000 years of penance. Removes sins and grants good rebirth.
donts: Avoid sleeping on bed (sleep on floor)
